question: Q1
claim: Composite convergence score is a fixed-weight linear blend of structural signals (research: questionCoverage*0.30 + claimVerificationRate*0.25 + (1-contradictionDensity)*0.15 + diversity*0.15 + depth*0.15).

question: Q1
claim: All research convergence signals are pure structural counts/ratios with no per-finding reliability or attester weight.

question: Q1
claim: bayesian-scorer.ts is a Laplace-smoothed (success+1)/(total+2) model-demotion helper, NOT the convergence scorer, and is not imported by convergence.cjs.

question: Q1
claim: Convergence is vote-count-floodable today: coverage needs only >=2 ANSWERS edges/question regardless of source reliability; weak findings inflate coverage/diversity/depth unbounded by attester quality.

question: Q1
claim: aionforge confidence is a bounded reliability-weighted Beta posterior: settles toward average attester quality, cannot be pushed to certainty by numbers; k attesters capped at (alpha+k)/(alpha+beta+k).

question: Q1
claim: Reliability is read not written by the attestation layer; unknown agent contributes uninformative 0.5; cold-start promotes nothing.

question: Q2
claim: findContradictions only lists CONTRADICTS edge pairs; no quarantine, no trust comparison, no victim suppression.

question: Q2
claim: Contradictions affect convergence only symmetrically (research contradictionDensity>0.15 blocker; review findingStability marks any CONTRADICTS-touching finding unstable); both sides stay live in recall.

question: Q2
claim: Contradictions are dropped into a symmetric penalty, not quarantined; no lower-trust victim is excluded while the higher-trust side survives.

question: Q2
claim: aionforge names a deterministic lower-trust victim; quarantines only when either side >= high_trust_threshold (0.7); recall excludes the contradiction source by edge presence so victim does not surface as current, nothing destroyed.

question: Q3
claim: fanout-salvage runSalvageSweep is post-hoc file recovery from captured stdout (else writes fanout_salvage_failed placeholder); no transient/fatal classification, no per-branch durable retry budget.

question: Q3
claim: aionforge classifies PassError::Transient (stay raw, retry as oldest-pending) vs Fatal/exceeded-max_retries(5) (marked failed, retained+audited); attempt count read from durable audit trail so crash gives no fresh retry budget.

question: Q2
claim: Adjudicator scoreVerdictDelta scores round-to-round stability across 5 change-magnitude dims (option_changed 0.35, confidence_delta 0.20, risk_jaccard 0.20, axis_flip 0.15, blocking_delta 0.10); no seat-reliability weighting.

question: Q4
claim: Cost guards are pure count ceilings (rounds 3/topics 5/seats 3 + saturation 0.2); evaluateCouncilCostGuards stops on counts+saturation+2 stable rounds; no live lag/pending/failed backpressure gauges.

question: Q4
claim: aionforge emits per-tick consolidation_lag_seconds / episodes_pending / episodes_failed gauges plus supersession/contradiction/quarantine counters.

question: Q6
claim: reduce-state.cjs updateStrategyContent calls replaceAnchorSection for 7 sections; replaceAnchorSection throws 'Missing anchor section <id> in strategy file' when the ANCHOR regex pair is absent.

question: Q6
claim: CONFIRMED BUG: shipped deep_research_strategy.md template has 13 headings + MACHINE-OWNED marker but ZERO ANCHOR markers; a fresh copy hard-fails 'Missing anchor section key-questions' on the first reduce after iteration 1.

question: Q6
note: This session's working research/deep-research-strategy.md carries the 7 anchor pairs (:24-87) because the driver hand-patched them after hitting the throw, corroborating the Known Context firsthand-hit note.

question: Q6
note: Fix: wrap each of the 7 machine-owned headings (key-questions §3, answered-questions §6, what-worked §7, what-failed §8, exhausted-approaches §9, ruled-out-directions §10, next-focus §11) in the template with its <!-- ANCHOR:id -->/<!-- /ANCHOR:id --> pair, mirroring the working strategy.md shape.

question: Q1
note: Dispatch named external/... at repo root but aionforge docs live under the parent spec folder 028-memory-search-intelligence/external/...; used the parent-scoped path.
